title: Smoke Signals

jokes.text: I fit my high school best friend into a suitcase way smaller than this when we were crossing the border between Laos and Vietnam
jokes.setup: Offered help with luggage
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions incredibly dark/illegal past experience as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis line - casually mentions human trafficking as if it's a helpful life skill. Excellent character integration and absurdist humor

jokes.text: Beach curls back. Yeah, you've mentioned that a couple times.
jokes.setup: Alexis repeating her excitement about beach curls
jokes.punchline: David's dry acknowledgment that she's obsessively mentioned this
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic moment - David's exasperation with Alexis's repetitive enthusiasm

jokes.text: Take it the 'Crows' movie is still shelved.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions Moira's had a 'relapse'
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact assessment of Moira's career disappointment
jokes.explanation: David's characteristic dry delivery makes Moira's dramatic career setbacks sound routine

jokes.text: You invited Dad. Okay, I don't know what that's supposed to mean, Alexis.
jokes.setup: David says they wanted to keep venue tour intimate
jokes.punchline: Alexis points out the obvious contradiction; David's defensive non-response
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - David caught in obvious hypocrisy, responds with characteristic deflection

jokes.text: John, remind me to reprimand that latch, it's been awfully moody today.
jokes.setup: Moira emerging from closet mishap
jokes.punchline: Moira anthropomorphizes a door latch with theatrical language
jokes.explanation: Pure Moira - dramatic vocabulary applied to mundane household problems, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Finally. I'll have the continental breakfast with the fresh-squeezed grapefruit juice. Orange Pekoe tea.
jokes.setup: Johnny brings David and Patrick to see Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira mistakes them for room service, still disoriented
jokes.explanation: Moira's confusion creates awkward moment while showing her diva expectations even when disoriented

jokes.text: Sadly, I won't be able to squire you for today's wedding venue peregrination.
jokes.setup: Moira needs to decline joining them
jokes.punchline: Uses unnecessarily pretentious vocabulary ('squire', 'peregrination') for simple concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary humor - 'peregrination' instead of 'trip' is perfectly pretentious

jokes.text: You invited Mom? She's literally been asleep in a closet for a week.
jokes.setup: Learning Moira was invited to venue tour
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact description of Moira's dramatic breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect David line - treats absurd situation as routine family dysfunction with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Take me with you.
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions going away for six months
jokes.punchline: Moira's desperate, pathetic plea
jokes.explanation: Moira's vulnerability breaking through her usual pretense creates uncomfortable but funny moment

jokes.text: David! I've asked you not to over indulge in that smoky cologne, it's enough to give someone a seizure!
jokes.setup: Moira smells smoke
jokes.punchline: Blames David's cologne in characteristically dramatic fashion
jokes.explanation: Moira's misdirected blame and hyperbolic health concerns are perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, this is not, this is not how I go! Somebody help! Moira!
jokes.setup: Moira trapped in closet by fire/smoke
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about her death while calling for help
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical response to danger - even in crisis, she's performing

jokes.text: I'm being annulled by a chancy door clasp!
jokes.setup: Moira trapped and panicking
jokes.punchline: Uses 'annulled' instead of 'killed' and 'chancy' for faulty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira vocabulary - 'annulled' and 'chancy' are perfectly wrong word choices that sound sophisticated

jokes.text: Jeez, Moira, I wait 'til at least 10:30 before I have my first beer.
jokes.setup: Stevie rescues disoriented Moira
jokes.punchline: Stevie's casual reference to her own drinking schedule while judging Moira
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - dry delivery while revealing her own questionable habits

jokes.text: You know, Moira, if Johnny locked you in that closet, we're going to have to call the police.
jokes.setup: Stevie helping confused Moira
jokes.punchline: Stevie's matter-of-fact consideration of domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Stevie's characteristic blunt assessment of the situation with no filter

jokes.text: My babies... My girls. Lorna, second from the left. If she takes on smoke, she'll never recover. And Cindy! Cindy, below her. Cindy, I just gave her a blowout.
jokes.setup: Moira referring to 'babies' and 'girls' suggests children
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be her wigs with individual names and styling history
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds tension about family safety then reveals Moira's vanity priorities. Perfect character moment

jokes.text: Yeah, it's the only venue for miles that doesn't look like a crime scene from a missing person docu-series.
jokes.setup: Patrick saying venue looks nice
jokes.punchline: David's characteristically dark and specific comparison
jokes.explanation: David's pessimistic worldview expressed through very specific pop culture reference

jokes.text: I thought that was a choice.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why no one told her about her show makeup
jokes.punchline: Patrick's diplomatic but cutting response
jokes.explanation: Patrick's polite burn delivered with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: I left Stevie double-fisting drinks in a hot tub at the cabaret wrap party last night. I think I saw you triple-fisting at one point.
jokes.setup: Explanation of why Stevie looks rough
jokes.punchline: Escalating from double-fisting to impossible triple-fisting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke building on Stevie's established party reputation

jokes.text: Oh no, not quite. God, no.
jokes.setup: Wedding venue host assumes David and Alexis are the couple
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous horrified rejection from both siblings
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling revulsion at being mistaken for a couple, well-timed simultaneous reaction

jokes.text: Gentlemen, please forgive me. I looked at your friend's face and thought someone had just come from her make up trial.
jokes.setup: Host realizing his mistake about the couple
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by insulting Alexis's appearance while apologizing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - character digs himself deeper while trying to recover

jokes.text: You look great.
jokes.setup: After Clive's insulting comment about Alexis
jokes.punchline: Patrick's supportive but awkwardly timed compliment
jokes.explanation: Patrick's characteristic kindness creating an awkward but sweet moment

jokes.text: See, David? Imagine if I wasn't here for this moment.
jokes.setup: David finding perfect venue
jokes.punchline: Alexis making the moment about herself after being excluded
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - turns David's emotional moment into validation of her own importance

jokes.text: Why do they call them toaster pops if it says on the box, 'Do not toast'?
jokes.setup: Roland explaining how he started a fire
jokes.punchline: Roland's confused logic about contradictory product naming
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland logic - genuinely confused by obvious things in endearingly dumb way

jokes.text: How mercurial is life? We all imagine being carried from the ashes by the Goddess Artemis and here I get a balatron from Barnum and Bailey.
jokes.setup: Moira reflecting on her rescue
jokes.punchline: Compares mythological rescue fantasy to being saved by circus clown (Roland)
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - classical references, pretentious vocabulary, and cutting insult wrapped in philosophical musing

jokes.text: I have been gifted a smoke signal and you will never again find me back in the closet.
jokes.setup: Moira declaring she's fixing the latches
jokes.punchline: Dramatic interpretation of literal smoke as spiritual message
jokes.explanation: Moira turning accidental closet entrapment into spiritual awakening with dramatic flair

jokes.text: I don't believe my career has been that kind to me. I all but perished in the name of an unrequited love for an industry that has burned me over and over again.
jokes.setup: Moira reflecting on her career
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of normal career setbacks as tragic romance
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical self-pity turning career disappointments into tragic operatic suffering

jokes.text: Oh, I have never needed less.
jokes.setup: Johnny offering support if she needs anything
jokes.punchline: Moira's declaration of complete independence immediately after being rescued from closet
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Moira claims independence moments after needing rescue, classic character blindness

jokes.text: And I am including that Peruvian ayahuasca retreat we embarked upon with Al and Tipper.
jokes.setup: Moira saying she's never felt more lucid
jokes.punchline: Comparison includes hallucinogenic drug experience with famous politicians
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist detail - casually mentions drug retreat with Al Gore and Tipper as point of comparison

jokes.text: You just remove those vetements, Mr. Rose, I'll be back in the shake of a lamb's rump.
jokes.setup: Moira suggesting skinny dipping
jokes.punchline: Uses pretentious 'vetements' and bizarre 'shake of a lamb's rump' instead of common phrases
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary - 'vetements' for clothes and mangled idiom 'lamb's rump' instead of 'lamb's tail'

jokes.text: Moira Rose is tech un-avail, she has a life to live.
jokes.setup: Moira calling her agent to retire
jokes.punchline: Pretentious mispronunciation of 'technically unavailable'
jokes.explanation: Moira's affected pronunciation of industry jargon makes her sound more pretentious than professional

jokes.text: Are these in dollars?
jokes.setup: Seeing wedding venue pricing
jokes.punchline: David's shocked question about currency
jokes.explanation: Good sticker shock joke - David's disbelief at prices expressed through currency question

jokes.text: Perhaps a copper package?
jokes.setup: Looking for cheaper option than bronze package
jokes.punchline: David suggesting even lower tier metal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the metal tier joke - copper is appropriately cheaper than bronze

jokes.text: You're clearly very desperate and I'm feeling terribly for you.
jokes.setup: Clive offering to help with pricing
jokes.punchline: Backhanded sympathy that insults while helping
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Clive's 'help' comes with unnecessary insult about desperation

jokes.text: Uh, I planned my ex girlfriend's intervention in less than an hour. Plus, it'll weed out all the dead weight.
jokes.setup: Patrick saying one month isn't enough time to plan
jokes.punchline: David's casual reference to planning intervention plus callous guest assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent David moment - casually mentions dark past while being pragmatically cruel about wedding guests

jokes.text: Um... I will not be here in one month, so I guess that makes me the dead weight?
jokes.setup: David saying short timeline will weed out dead weight
jokes.punchline: Alexis realizing she's being called dead weight
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - Alexis realizes the cruel implication of David's comment

jokes.text: I once dated someone who left me for a stuffed animal. I'm not having my big day... Our... Our big day compromised because Alexis wanted to chill in a tankini for six months.
jokes.setup: David defending his wedding priorities
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd past rejection then corrects possessive pronoun while insulting Alexis's trip
jokes.explanation: Peak David - absurd relationship history, self-correction showing Patrick's influence, and dismissive description of Alexis's scientific research

jokes.text: And I would never wear a tankini!
jokes.setup: David's insult about tankini
jokes.punchline: Alexis more offended by fashion accusation than actual insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis priorities - more upset about fashion slur than being called dead weight

jokes.text: I might have overreacted and for that, I am apologetic. So, you're sorry. I knew there was a reason I came on this trip. My God. I am remorseful over an action I participated in.
jokes.setup: David attempting to apologize
jokes.punchline: David's elaborate non-apology getting progressively more verbose and less sincere
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David character work - completely unable to say 'sorry', each attempt gets more ridiculous and clinical

jokes.text: Oh, I'm not wearing them on the plane, they hand out slippers with the mimosas. When was the last time you flew economy?
jokes.setup: Patrick asking why she's wearing heels on plane
jokes.punchline: Alexis reveals she doesn't know about economy class amenities
jokes.explanation: Good class privilege joke - Alexis genuinely doesn't understand economy flying

jokes.text: Oh, John, I can just see you aimlessly wandering through life. Stoic but stymied, not a friend in the world and so very lost.
jokes.setup: Moira reflecting on nearly dying
jokes.punchline: Paints devastatingly bleak picture of Johnny's hypothetical widowhood
jokes.explanation: Dark Moira humor - romanticizes his potential grief while accidentally revealing harsh truths about his social life

jokes.text: I lost my Patek Philippe in that lake, but I wouldn't have had it any other way.
jokes.setup: Johnny reminiscing about romantic skinny dipping
jokes.punchline: Mentions losing extremely expensive watch but claims it was worth it
jokes.explanation: Good Johnny moment - shows his wealth background while being genuinely romantic

jokes.text: You could be hit by a Mack truck, or bopped on the head by a tiny piece of space debris!
jokes.setup: Moira arguing they should seize the moment
jokes.punchline: Escalates from realistic to absurdly specific death scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to bizarre death scenarios, 'bopped' is perfectly silly verb choice

jokes.text: I've actually been jealous of your trip. Like, you're getting out there, seeing the world.
jokes.setup: Stevie drunk and honest
jokes.punchline: Admits vulnerability while acknowledging her own drinking
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment for Stevie - rare vulnerability while drunk creates genuine sweetness

jokes.text: your flight's not leaving for another month. Yeah, but it's day before month, so you're not leaving on July 8th, you're leaving on August 7th.
jokes.setup: Alexis can't check in for flight
jokes.punchline: Stevie discovers Alexis misread date format
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - explains plot while showing Alexis's privileged incompetence with basic tasks

jokes.text: Oh, well, what would the back end look like on a deal like that? Oh, I see, what an emphatically Kismetic turn of events.
jokes.setup: Moira on phone with agent
jokes.punchline: Uses business jargon then mispronounces 'kismet' as 'Kismetic'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary error - 'Kismetic' sounds medical/scientific instead of mystical

jokes.text: I found your husband sunning himself in the creek, like he was in some Grecian bath.
jokes.setup: Officer explaining Johnny's arrest
jokes.punchline: Describes Johnny's nude creek behavior as pretentious European spa experience
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - Johnny's attempt at romantic gesture seen through cop's eyes becomes absurd

jokes.text: Officer, perhaps we could arrange a plea bargain?
jokes.setup: Johnny arrested for indecent exposure
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately jumps to legal negotiation
jokes.explanation: Moira's dramatic response treats minor incident like serious legal case

jokes.text: I promise to keep my husband habilimented from now on.
jokes.setup: Moira promising Johnny will stay clothed
jokes.punchline: Uses pretentious 'habilimented' instead of 'clothed'
jokes.explanation: Typical Moira vocabulary - formal, pretentious word for simple concept

jokes.text: The algae was so slippery, the cop had to throw me his fishing line.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining his struggle getting out of creek
jokes.punchline: Reveals he needed rescue from police fishing line
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy detail - adds humiliation to Johnny's romantic gesture gone wrong

jokes.text: Well, button back up your birthday tux, John, because I've just found out the 'Crows' movie is back in business!
jokes.setup: Moira got call about her movie
jokes.punchline: References Johnny's nude state with theatrical 'birthday tux'
jokes.explanation: Clever Moira phrase - 'birthday tux' for naked state is perfectly theatrical

jokes.text: Clive didn't have jumper cables because he doesn't drive because he's scared of car horns.
jokes.setup: Explaining why their car situation can't be easily fixed
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd chain of Clive's phobias preventing simple solution
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how minor phobias create major inconveniences

jokes.text: The truth is, is that I'm really gonna miss you. Thank you. I'm going to miss you, as well.
jokes.setup: Siblings having rare honest moment
jokes.punchline: David's formal, stilted response to emotional honesty
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - David's formal language reveals difficulty with emotion

jokes.text: Like that time you showed up to Kate Winslet's wedding a month late. The calligraphy was hard to read and Billy Zane also did the same thing, David.
jokes.setup: David mocking Alexis for date confusion
jokes.punchline: Alexis defends herself with celebrity wedding story and Billy Zane reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent Alexis backstory - casually mentions celebrity connections while Billy Zane reference is perfectly random

jokes.text: They process their pork on the first Sunday of every month, hence our discounted rates.
jokes.setup: Horrible pig processing sounds during romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Clive's matter-of-fact explanation of the horrific timing
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - explains the disturbing sounds and discount in businesslike manner

jokes.text: Like a thousand guinea pigs were being shoved into a wood chipper.
jokes.setup: Describing the horrible pig processing sounds
jokes.punchline: David's characteristically dark and specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic David dark imagery - specific and disturbing comparison delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Well, it sounds to me like you two dodged a bacon-wrapped bullet. I'll say it again: life is but a strand of happy accidents.
jokes.setup: Roland offering wisdom about the venue disaster
jokes.punchline: Food pun plus claiming he's said profound phrase before when he hasn't
jokes.explanation: Good Roland moment - bad pun plus false claim of wisdom, perfectly in character

jokes.text: You've never said that before.
jokes.setup: Roland claiming he's said his wisdom before
jokes.punchline: David's flat correction
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff - David calling out Roland's false claim with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: he still hasn't lizard-proofed his place and I have absolutely no desire to be there until that happens.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why delayed trip is fine
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions bizarre requirement for boyfriend's home
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - treats absurd requirement as completely reasonable due to her phobias

jokes.text: Oh, in other news: your father was arrested for indecent exposure. Ew, I'm eating!
jokes.setup: Moira casually mentioning Johnny's arrest
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted response while eating
jokes.explanation: Good family dysfunction moment - Moira's casual delivery of shocking news, David's typical reaction

tmdb_overview: David scouts a potential wedding venue, while Johnny and Moira have a romantic picnic by the creek.
insight: Season finale opener leans character comedy over punchlines, establishing emotional stakes.
